questions.question: Explain the difference between bounded and unbounded data in the context of data processing.
expectedPoints: Bounded data is finite with a known beginning and end.
It is typically processed using batch processing (e.g., a day's worth of log files).
Unbounded data is a continuous, infinite stream of events.
It is typically processed using stream processing (e.g., live sensor data from an IoT device).

questions.question: Compare and contrast the Lambda and Kappa architectures. What are the advantages of the Kappa approach?
expectedPoints: Lambda uses a batch layer for comprehensive historical processing and a speed layer for real-time processing.
It requires maintaining two separate codebases for the same logic.
The serving layer merges results from both layers.
Kappa uses a single stream processing pipeline for all data.
Historical data is reprocessed by replaying from the stream log (e.g., Kafka).
Advantages of Kappa: Simplified architecture, single codebase to maintain, avoids synchronization between batch and speed layers.
